__TR_why_41__: The 3 False Beliefs That Cost Buyers Money
__TR_why_43__: "The seller's churn methodology is industry-standard, so the number is reliable."
__TR_why_45__: There is no standard. Every seller makes methodology choices that flatter their number. The only reliable number is the one you compute from raw data.
__TR_why_47__: "I'm not technical enough to analyze raw subscription data myself."
__TR_why_49__: You don't need to be. You need the CSV and a tool that computes churn five ways. The analysis takes minutes, not a finance degree.
__TR_why_4__: Why I Built Churn Lens — The $340K Lesson
__TR_why_51__: "The seller wouldn't hide churn from me — they'd get caught in the earnout."
__TR_why_53__: Most churn methodology choices are legal, defensible, and invisible. Earnouts don't protect you from legitimate-looking accounting that understates real churn by 4x.
__TR_why_55__: Get the 23-point buyer-side churn audit checklist. Ask the seller for the raw CSV before you trust any summary number.
